rulings.Case Title: INFAC MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, VS. COMDISCO INVESTMENT GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Reopen Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 25, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 8, Plaintiff's Motion To Reopen Discovery Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff INFAC MANAGEMENT CORP's Motion to Re-Open Discovery and Compel Additional Depositions of Defendant VERNON LOUCKS and CHARLES LOUCKS is GRANTED. The subsequent deposition of defendant VERNON LOUCKS shall go forward on or before February 22, 2013, and shall be limited as follows: 1) to one and a half hours on the record; 2) to the subject of the documents produced by Defendants on June 29, 2012; and 3) the deposition may go forward telephonically. The subsequent deposition of defendant CHARLES LOUCKS shall go forward on or before February 22, 2013, and shall be limited as follows: 1) to one and a half hours on the record; 2) to the subject of the documents produced by Defendants on June 29, 2012; 3) the deposition may go forward telephonically. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: CHERIE R SAFAPOU PHD VS. MOSY DOROODIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 25, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANT MOSY DOROODIAN's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The anti-SLAPP motion to strike is granted. Defendant demonstrates that plaintiff's causes of action arise from petitioning activity in connection with Defendant's production of evidence and testimony at judicial proceedings. (See First Amended Complaint page 9, line 28 - p. 10, line 3; page 12, lines 1-6; p. 13, lines 4-15.) Plaintiff contends that defendant made oral and written statements in connection with a marital settlement, including providing these statements and documents to the court. The second cause of action for "breach of obligation through intentional misrepresentation and deceit, etc." incorporates all of the factual allegations from the first cause of action, and includes allegations that Defendant "produced his fraudulent handiwork to the Court at the April 9, 2009 hearing in order to deceive the Court" about written agreements between Plaintiff and Defendant regarding their marital settlement that are attached to the Amended Complaint. Pages 19-24 of the Amended Complaint state that they are "offered for their historical background only, as they are time-barred and inadmissible as fraud claims of their own," and thus are not addressed by the Court. The burden shifted to Plaintiff to show a probability of prevailing on her claims, which she has failed to do. Civil Code section 47(b) provides an absolute privilege for publication made in a judicial proceeding. Further, plaintiff's declarations do not show a likelihood of prevailing. Her declaration does not demonstrate "fraud." Further, Plaintiff admits that she was suffering from the influence of psychological maladies and was under the influence of powerful medications at the time. (Supplement Declaration In Support of Amended Opposition to Motion to Strike, filed Nov. 16, 2012, at page 13.) The Court grants Plaintiff's request for judicial notice. Defendant may make a motion for fees per CCP 425.16(c). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: NATRIXIS FACTOR, A CORPORATION VS. 2163 UNION CORPORATION, A CORPORATION, DBA NIDA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 25, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANT 2163 UNION CORPORATION, A CORPORATION, DBA NIDA's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. The motion is denied. Defendant fails to meet its burden of showing demonstrate mistake, excusable neglect, inadvertence, or surprise within the meaning of CCP 473. It is undisputed that Defendant was properly served with the summons on May 21. 2012. There is no explanation as to why Defendant's employee did not provide the summons to its president Mr. Giobbio, or why Defendant did not otherwise take any action upon receiving the summons. Defendant was served with a request for entry of default on July 26, 2012. Mr. Giobbio admits he personally received the summons and complaint in August 2012 (apparently from his office), but failed to act on it until mid- September 2012 when he returned to San Francisco. There is no explanation as to why Defendant did not receive a copy of the request for entry of default in August at the same time he received the summons and complaint, since it had been served. He did not take any steps to discuss the summons and complaint and request for entry of default with his attorney until September 17, 2012. On these facts, the requisite showing for relief from default has not been made. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BRIAN CARTMELL et al VS. BITCOINICA LP, ALSO KNOWN AS BITCOINICA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dimsiss Or To Stay Action; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 25, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANTs INTERSANGO LTD. and PATRICK STRATEMAN's Motion To Dimsiss Or To Stay Action; Request For Judicial Notice. Denied. Moving Defendants have failed to show that New Zealand is a suitable forum. It has not been shown that Defendants Intersango Ltd, Donald Norman, or Amir Taaki are subject to New Zealand jurisdiction. Additionally, Defendant has presented no evidence as to the applicable statute of limitations in New Zealand and whether Plaintiffs' claims are time barred in that forum. Even if a suitable forum did exist, the private and public interest factors do not warrant granting the motion. As to the private factors: Plaintiffs did not agree to adjudicate all disputes in New Zealand. The language of the Terms and Conditions allegedly on the website, even if it is enforceable, states only that "you [meaning a customer] agree to submit to the personal and exclusive jurisdiction of the court located within New Zealand to settle any dispute," not that customers could not file actions against Bitcoinica outside New Zealand. None of the individual defendants reside in New Zealand. There has been no showing that access to evidence requires moving the case to New Zealand. Defendant Strateman apparently accessed relevant documents regarding claims while in California. (See Powell Decl. in opposition to motion to quash and Skyp conversation..) California has a strong interest in assuring a forum for California residents. Two of the four plaintiffs reside in California. The causes of action are based upon contract, common count, and tort liability. Moving Defendants' argument that the California courts are overburdened is not, under all of the circumstances, a basis to move this case filed by California residents to New Zealand. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
